---
title: "Antarctic Seafloor Annotated Imagery Database - Classification Guide"
author: "Edited by Jan Jansen"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
# site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
# bibliography: [book.bib, packages.bib]
# url: your book url like https://bookdown.org/yihui/bookdown
# cover-image: path to the social sharing image like images/cover.jpg
# link-citations: yes
# github-repo: rstudio/bookdown-demo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
options(scipen=999)
library(imager)
r.dir <- "R:/IMAS/Antarctic_Seafloor/Clean_Data_For_Permanent_Storage/AnnotationLibrary_AllFinishedSurveys/"
img.cover.dir <- paste0(r.dir,"Cover/")

## load annotation file
load(paste0(img.cover.dir,"Circumpolar_DownwardImages_PointScore_Annotations_202312.Rdata"))
ann.cover.dat$Name_to_publish <- as.factor(ann.cover.dat$Name_to_publish)
#ann.cover.dat$AMC <- as.factor(ann.cover.dat$AMC)
ann.cover.dat$img.path <- paste0(ann.cover.dat$Folder.path,ann.cover.dat$Name)

all.coralNet <- levels(ann.cover.dat$Name_to_publish)
#all.AMC <- levels(ann.cover.dat$AMC)
idx.vme.coralNet <- c(c(1:8), c(13:26), 30, 37, 41, 47, 56, 57, 58, c(73:84), c(89:112), 133:134)
vme.coralNet <- all.coralNet[idx.vme.coralNet]
#vme.AMC <- all.AMC[idx.vme.coralNet]

## create function to:
### load images for each label
### plot them in a nice way
load_and_plot <- function(label.idx=1, img.count=9){  ## default is 6 example images
  ## select the labels to display
  #lab.AMC <- levels(ann.cover.dat$AMC)[label.idx]
  lab <- levels(ann.cover.dat$Name_to_publish)[label.idx]
   ## is that label a VME
  if(lab%in%vme.coralNet){
    vme = "Yes"
  }else vme = "No"
  ## subset to only annotations with that label
  lab.dat <- ann.cover.dat[which(ann.cover.dat$Name_to_publish==lab),]
  ## how many are there?
  l <- nrow(lab.dat)
  ## if examples come from multiple surveys, use at least 1 image from each survey
  lab.surveys <- unique(lab.dat$SurveyID)
  lab.dat.s <- which(lab.dat$SurveyID==lab.surveys[1])
  s <- sample(lab.dat.s,1)
  if(length(lab.surveys)>1){
     for(j in 2:length(lab.surveys)){
      lab.dat.s <- which(lab.dat$SurveyID==lab.surveys[j])
      s <- c(s,sample(lab.dat.s,1))
    }
  }
  ## now fill up examples to a maximum of 9 per label
  if(length(s)>=img.count){ ## if there are 9 or more surveys
    sel <- sample(s, img.count)
  } else if(l==length(s)){ ## if there is only one label per survey
    sel <- s
  }else if(l < 9){ ## if we have fewer than 9 images for a label
    sel <- c(s, sample((1:l)[-s],l-length(s)))
  } else sel <- c(s, sample((1:l)[-s],img.count-length(s))) ## and all other cases
  ## subset data
  temp.dat <- lab.dat[sel,]
  ## labels to display
  cat(paste0("### ",unique(temp.dat$Name_to_publish)," {-} \n \n"))
  print(knitr::kable(data.frame("CATAMI"=unique(temp.dat$CATAMI),
                                "AMC_ID"=unique(temp.dat$AMC_ID),
                                "CAAB"=unique(temp.dat$CAAB),
                                "Found_in_surveys"=paste(unique(lab.dat$SurveyID), collapse=", "),
                                "Total_annotations"= l,
                                "VME_taxa"=vme)
  ))
  ## define path to library images
  locs <- list.files(paste0(img.cover.dir,unique(temp.dat$Label_after_review)), full.names=TRUE)
  ## plot example images
  par(mfrow=c(3,3), oma=c(1,0,1,0), mar=c(2,0,0,0))
  if(l < 9){z <- 1:l
  }else z <- 1:img.count
  for(k in z){
    plot(load.image(locs[k]), axes=FALSE)
    mtext(temp.dat$TransectID[k],side=1, cex=0.7)
  }
  cat(" \n \n")
}
```

# Point-score classification catalog {-}

This Classification Catalog shows example images for all labels used for the point-scoring method in the Antarctic Seafloor Annotated Imagery Database (AS-AID). It aims to give users an overview of the labels with reference to matching CATAMI labels. This catalog represents a snapshot of the database at the time of publication of the associated data-paper.  

Up to nine randomly selected images are presented for each classification, alongside a table with the following information:  
  
* CATAMI: matching label in the CATAMI classification. For more information, the CATAMI paper can be found under this [link](https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0141039)  
* AMC_ID: matching identifier in the Australian Morphospecies Catalog, and extension of the CATAMI classification.  
* CAAB codes: [Codes for Australian Aquatic Biota](https://www.cmar.csiro.au/data/caab/)  
* Found_in_surveys: a list of all surveys in AS-AID in which this organism has been detected  
* Total_annotations: the total number of annotations for this label  
* VME-taxa: whether this label is identified as a CCAMLR Vulnerable Marine Ecosystem taxa  
  
Please direct any enquiries, questions or comments to Jan.Jansen@utas.edu.au
